title: Istio Gateway vs Kubernetes Ingress
description:
## Istio Gateway vs Kubernetes Ingress

Istio provides its own `Gateway` resource as an alternative to the standard Kubernetes `Ingress`.

Which of the following is a key advantage of using an Istio `Gateway` + `VirtualService` over a plain Kubernetes `Ingress`?

options.text: Istio Gateway is cheaper to operate than an Ingress controller
Istio Gateway supports mTLS termination, traffic mirroring, weighted routing, and fault injection that standard Ingress cannot express
Kubernetes Ingress requires a CRD installation; Istio Gateway is built in
Istio Gateway automatically creates DNS records, whereas Ingress does not

explanation: Istio Gateway exposes a port on the Ingress Gateway pod but delegates all routing rules to a VirtualService. This unlocks the full power of Istio traffic management at the edge: mTLS termination, fault injection, traffic mirroring, weighted canary routing, header-based routing, retries, and timeouts — none of which are expressible in standard Kubernetes Ingress annotations in a portable way.

hints.title: Gateway selects the proxy, VirtualService defines routes
hints.body: A Gateway resource binds to a specific label selector (usually the Istio ingress gateway pod) and opens a port. A VirtualService then references that Gateway to apply routing rules.
